lesson_title: Quiz on accessing Claude with the API

questions.question_text: You want to send a request to Claude's API. What's the minimum information you must include?

options.text: Only the API key and your question
API key, model name, messages, and max tokens

questions.question_text: You ask Claude 'What is pizza?' and it answers. Then you ask 'What toppings are popular?' but Claude doesn't understand what you're referring to. What's the problem?
When Claude processes your text, what's the first thing it does?

options.text: Breaks it into smaller chunks called tokens

questions.question_text: Users complain your chat app feels slow because they wait 20 seconds staring at a loading spinner, then all the generated text appears at once. What can fix this?
You're building a web app that talks to Claude. Where should you store your API key?

options.text: In your mobile app that users install
In a text file on the user's computer
On your server that users can't access
In your JavaScript code that users download

questions.question_text: You're building a math tutor bot. You want Claude to give hints instead of direct answers. What should you use?

options.text: Setting a very low word limit
Using all capital letters in your messages
A system prompt explaining the tutor role
Asking users to be more specific

questions.question_text: You want Claude to give very predictable, consistent answers for a factual Q&A app. What temperature setting should you use?
You're building an app that needs clean JSON from Claude with no extra text or formatting. How do you get just the raw JSON?

options.text: Send the request multiple times and pick the best one
Ask Claude very nicely to only return JSON
Combine prefilled messages and stop sequences
Use a very high temperature setting
